source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
<image_1> In the transition-state structure shown in the image, calculate the total number of bonds in the structure, including single, double, and triple bonds but excluding those involving hydrogen.

Note: Disregard arrows. Consider all components present in the transition-state structure shown in the image.
extraction_notes: No explicit image path or URL is present; only an in-memory image object appears in `image_1`.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the image' and asks about the transition-state structure present there
the target quantity depends on counting visual bonds across all components in the diagram
multi_step: the task requires identifying all non-hydrogen bonds in a chemistry diagram and summing single, double, and triple bonds
question_text adds filtering rules: exclude bonds involving hydrogen, disregard arrows, and include all components present
this is more than direct lookup because it combines visual parsing with constrained counting, but it is still a relatively bounded procedure
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ('13')
the question defines a concrete counting rule, so a reviewer can verify by recounting the structure in the image
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: question text, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are still recoverable
the sample is highly image-dependent, and the only recorded risk flag is small image resolution
registry passed and verifiability is strong, but the image-quality risk makes follow-up preferable to immediate keep
